description_long: This induction cooker features a modern, rectangular design with a black glass-ceramic cooktop and a stainless steel front panel. The cooktop has four distinct circular heating zones, with one highlighted in red, and a central control panel consisting of several round knobs and buttons. The front face includes a large, transparent oven door with a metallic handle and two visible racks inside. The sides and back of the cooker are metallic, with one side displaying a detailed circuit board and a set of vertical pipes, suggesting an industrial or repurposed context. The top surface also shows a paper sheet and a grill vent, adding to its urban, possibly upcycled appearance. The overall look is robust, with a mix of clean and weathered textures, making it suitable for outdoor or semi-industrial urban environments.
description: A robust induction cooker with a black glass-ceramic cooktop, four burners, a stainless steel oven front, and industrial side details including a circuit board and pipes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the oven door with a metallic handle, transparent glass, and the cooktop with control knobs and burners above.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the metallic, slightly weathered surface and a clear view of the cooktop's burners and control panel.
description_view_2: Rear view featuring a detailed circuit board and vertical pipes, with the cooktop and a paper sheet visible on top.
description_view_3: Right side view showing a weathered metallic surface and the cooktop with a paper sheet and burners visible.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel is centrally located on the cooktop, featuring a row of round knobs and buttons arranged horizontally. There are no visible digital displays or indicator lights, and the controls are flanked by the heating zones. Labels or icons are not clearly visible.
